site_goal: Provide a dense, dashboard-style hub where amateur storm chasers can monitor live doppler, file chase logs, review gear, coordinate spotter networks, and connect via callsign-based identity.
audience: ham radio operators with weather interest
description: TORNADO ALLEY OPERATIONS NETWORK ('TAON-1') — an experimental utilitarian web app styled like a 1990s NOAA workstation crossed with a ham radio packet cluster. The entire UI runs in radar-green IBM Plex Mono on near-black (#050807), with amber alert orange used only for WATCH/WARNING states and clickable affordances. Layout is grid-heavy with thin 1px borders, ASCII separators, and live-updating numeric readouts. Every page header carries a 'PACKET' status line: UTC clock, current SPC outlook category, active chaser count, and last-strike timestamp. Multi-column information density is the point — sidebars, ticker bars, and inline mini-radars are everywhere. No hero images, no marketing fluff; instead, embedded GR2Analyst-style radar tiles, sparkline gauges, and tables of CAPE/Helicity/SRH values. Callsigns (e.g. 'KC5-WHIRL', 'N0-SCUD') are the primary identity throughout. The tone is field-operator: terse, abbreviation-heavy, with footer disclaimers about not interpreting outputs as official NWS guidance.
motif: monospace dashboard with ASCII rules, radar sweeps, callsign tags, and amber WATCH/WARNING chips

pages.layout_pattern: four-quadrant operations console: top-left live CONUS doppler mosaic, top-right SPC convective outlook map with day-1/2/3 tabs, bottom-left ticker of recent member chase pings (callsign + lat/lon + time), bottom-right rolling LSR (Local Storm Reports) feed; persistent top status bar with UTC clock, sun/moon, and dewpoint readouts

tabular log archive: filter rail (year, state, EF-rating, member callsign, storm mode), main table with sortable columns (date, location, intercept time, storm type, max hail, peak wind, distance driven), expandable row reveals embedded GPS track, hodograph snapshot, and narrative entry

review database styled as a parts catalog: left nav by category (mobile mesonets, anemometers, hail guards, mesh radios, dashcams, laptops, lightning detectors), center grid of reviewed items with mini-spec readouts, right-rail comparison cart that holds up to four items for side-by-side spec diff

regional forum index: collapsible accordion of NWS County Warning Areas (CWAs) as forum boards, each row shows post count, last-callsign-active, and a tiny activity sparkline; threaded discussion view uses monospace with quoted reply ancestry indented with '>' marks

month grid where each day cell color-codes SPC categorical risk (MRGL/SLGT/ENH/MOD/HIGH) with amber escalation; clicking a day opens a side panel with hazard breakdown (tornado/wind/hail probabilities), participating chasers, and rendezvous waypoints

dense masonry of supercell photographs on black with thin amber borders; each tile carries an EXIF strip (lens, focal length, GPS, time, callsign of photographer) and storm metadata (mesocyclone tag, RFD slot, lowering type); filter chips for 'wall cloud', 'inflow band', 'mammatus', 'LP', 'HP', 'classic'
sections: masonry photo grid with EXIF strips

pages.layout_pattern: callsign-driven member directory: searchable table by callsign, real name (optional), home CWA, vehicle ID, certifications (SKYWARN, ARRL, HAM class), and last-active-on-net timestamp; profile detail view shows chase mileage total, storm-mode preferences, and rig schematic

message_intent: signal operational seriousness and field credibility
make dense real-time data legible at a glance
foreground callsign identity over real-name social patterns
communicate that this is community-run, not an official NWS service
interactions: gear comparison cart up to four items
photo lightbox with radar overlay toggle
mobile_behavior: quadrants stack vertically into a single scrollable column; persistent packet status bar collapses to UTC clock + active chaser count; tables become horizontally scrollable with sticky callsign column; gear comparison cart becomes a bottom sheet
